settings.propertyDescription: The customer ID of the tenant in which to execute the integration. For use in multi-tenant (MSSP) environments.

settings.value: epo.device.r epo.device.w epo.evt.r epo.taggroup.r epo.taggroup.w epo.tags.r epo.tags.w mi.user.investigate soc.inv.ade
epo.device.r epo.device.w epo.evt.r epo.taggroup.r epo.taggroup.w epo.tags.r epo.tags.w mi.user.investigate soc.inv.ade

settings.propertyDescription: The Microsoft Entra endpoint to connect to.
The Microsoft Graph endpoint to connect to.
The client (application) ID of the Microsoft Entra application to use in the integration.
The client secret value of the Microsoft Entra app to use in the integration.
The Microsoft Entra ID (tenant ID) value.
The default mailbox to use in the integration.
If enabled, user's mailbox address will be fetched from the 'mail' attribute of user's details. Otherwise, user's mailbox address will be fetched from the 'userPrincipalName' field.
Specify a base64 encoded private key that will be used to decrypt the email.
Specify a base64 encoded certificate that will be used to decrypt the email.
Specify a base64 encoded trusted CA certificate for signature verification.
If selected, the integration verifies that the SSL certificate for the connection to the Microsoft Graph server is valid.

settings.value: epo.device.r epo.device.w epo.evt.r epo.taggroup.r epo.taggroup.w epo.tags.r epo.tags.w mi.user.investigate soc.inv.ade

settings.propertyDescription: UI root of the Chronicle instance. It will be used to create a link that points back to Chronicle across multiple actions.
API root of the Chronicle instance.

Service Account that is used for authentication. You can configure either this parameter or the Workload Identity Email parameter. If both this and Workload Identity Email not provided, the default Service Account of the SecOps Instance will be used to authenticate.

The client email address of your workload identity. You can configure either this parameter or the User's Service Account parameter. To impersonate service accounts with the workload identity email address, grant the Service Account Token Creator role to your service account. If both this and User's Service Account not provided, the default Service Account of the SecOps Instance will be used to authenticate.
If enabled, verify the SSL certificate for the connection to the Google Chronicle server is valid.

settings.value: epo.device.r epo.device.w epo.evt.r epo.taggroup.r epo.taggroup.w epo.tags.r epo.tags.w mi.user.investigate soc.inv.ade
